perturbation.original_text: 3. Compensation to Company. In recognition of the fact that Company will respond to inquiries that otherwise would be handled by FIIOC, FIIOC agrees to pay Company a quarterly fee computed as follows: At the close of each calendar quarter, FIIOC will determine the Average Daily Assets held in the Funds by the Company. Average Daily Assets shall be the sum of the daily assets for each calendar day in the quarter divided by the number of calendar days in the quarter. The Average Daily Assets shall be multiplied by [ ] ([ ] basis points) and that sum shall be divided by four. The resulting number shall be the quarterly fee for that quarter, which shall be paid to Company during the following month. Should any Participation Agreement(s) between Company and any Fund(s) be terminated effective before the last day of a quarter, Company shall be entitled to a fee for that portion of the quarter during which the Participation Agreement was still in effect, unless such termination is due to misconduct on the part of the Company. For such a stub quarter, Average Daily Assets shall be the sum of the daily assets for each calendar day in the quarter through and including the date of termination of the Participation Agreement(s), divided by the number of calendar days in that quarter for which the Participation Agreement was in effect. Such Average Daily Assets shall be multiplied by [ ] ([ ] basis points) and that number shall be multiplied by the number of days in such quarter that the Participation Agreement was in effect, then divided by three hundred sixty-five. The resulting number shall be the quarterly fee for the stub quarter, which shall be paid to Company during the following month. Notwithstanding the foregoing, compensation for each calendar quarter will not exceed [ ].
perturbation.changed_text: 3. Compensation to Company. In recognition of the fact that Company will respond to inquiries that otherwise would be handled by FIIOC, FIIOC agrees to pay Company a quarterly fee. The specific amount and calculation of this quarterly fee will be determined at FIIOC's sole discretion.
perturbation.explanation: The original text defines a clear calculation for the quarterly fee, based on Average Daily Assets and basis points. The modified text replaces this with a statement that the fee will be 'determined at FIIOC's sole discretion'. This creates an internal contradiction. The agreement states there is a 'quarterly fee', implying a predictable compensation structure, but then gives FIIOC complete control over the fee amount, which contradicts the expectation of a calculated or pre-agreed sum. This affects the enforcement because it creates uncertainty around how much compensation the company should expect to receive.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. Compensation to Company. In recognition of the fact that Company will respond to inquiries that otherwise would be handled by FIIOC, FIIOC agrees to pay Company a quarterly fee. The specific amount and calculation of this quarterly fee will be determined at FIIOC's sole discretion.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified a formula for calculating the quarterly fee. The changed text replaces this with a statement that the fee will be determined at FIIOC's sole discretion. This directly contradicts the expectation of a calculated or pre-agreed sum, creating a strong in-text contradiction because it makes the fee calculation unpredictable and unenforceable as per the original agreement. The changed text essentially invalidates the contradicted text by giving FIIOC complete control over the fee amount, creating uncertainty around how much compensation the company should expect to receive. This falls under 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: 1. Information to be Provided to Company. FIIOC agrees to provide to Company, on a periodic basis, directly or through a designee, information about the Funds' investment objectives, investment policies, portfolio holdings, performance, etc. The content and format of such information shall be as FIIOC, in its sole discretion, shall choose. FIIOC may change the format and/or content of such informational reports, and the frequency with which such information is provided. For purposes of Section 4.2 of each of the Company's Participation Agreement(s) with the Funds, FIIOC represents that it is the designee of the Funds, and Company may therefore use the information provided by FIIOC without seeking additional permission from the Funds.
perturbation.changed_text: 1. Information to be Provided to Company. FIIOC agrees to provide to Company, on a periodic basis, directly or through a designee, information about the Funds. The content and format of such information shall be determined by mutual agreement of both parties.
perturbation.explanation: The original text specifies that FIIOC has sole discretion over the content, format, and frequency of the information provided. The modified text states the content and format 'shall be determined by mutual agreement of both parties'. This creates a direct contradiction. If the contract relies on FIIOC's ability to unilaterally decide on information delivery, enforcement becomes problematic. For example, if a later section references information formats established by FIIOC, it clashes with the requirement for mutual agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. Information to be Provided to Company. FIIOC agrees to provide to Company, on a periodic basis, directly or through a designee, information about the Funds. The content and format of such information shall be determined by mutual agreement of both parties.
perturbation.justification: The modified text states that the content and format of information provided by FIIOC shall be determined by mutual agreement of both parties. This directly contradicts the original agreement, where FIIOC had sole discretion. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text, emphasizing that the change itself introduces the contradiction. This change breaks compatibility with any clauses that rely on FIIOC's sole decision-making power regarding information delivery.
